introText: Alabama's Medicaid program is called Alabama Medicaid Agency, administered at the county level. programs.eligibility: Nursing home level of care needed

faqs.question: What counts as income for Alabama Medicaid eligibility?
faqs.answer: Alabama Medicaid Agency counts Social Security, pensions, SSI, and most earned income toward eligibility. For most adults, income must fall below 138% of the Federal Poverty Level (about $1,732/month for an individual in 2026).

faqs.question: How do I apply for Medicaid in Alabama?
faqs.answer: You can apply online, by phone, by mail, or in person at your local county office. Processing typically takes 30-45 days.

introText: Medi-Cal is California's Medicaid program. Pomona, CA residents apply through their local county office, with 30–45 day processing. Below are 2026 income and asset limits and the documents you need.

applicationMethods.description: Apply online through the California Medicaid portal
Call the Pomona Department of Social Services
Visit your local Pomona county office

programs.name: Aged, Blind, and Disabled
programs.description: Coverage for seniors 65+ in Pomona with disabilities
programs.eligibility: Income below SSI limits

programs.name: Home and Community-Based Services
programs.description: Waiver program for in-home care in Pomona as an alternative to nursing facilities
programs.eligibility: Nursing home level of care needed

faqs.question: What are the Medi-Cal income limits in Pomona, CA?
faqs.answer: In Pomona, Medi-Cal income limits follow the statewide California guidelines — generally 138% of the Federal Poverty Level for most adults. Seniors 65+ may have different limits. Contact the Pomona county office for exact figures.

faqs.question: Where can I apply for Medi-Cal in Pomona?
faqs.answer: Apply online through the California Medicaid portal, by phone, or in person at the Pomona county Department of Social Services. Processing typically takes 30-45 days.

faqs.question: Does Medi-Cal cover home care in Pomona?
faqs.answer: Yes, Medi-Cal may cover home and community-based services in Pomona through waiver programs. These allow eligible seniors to receive care at home rather than in a nursing facility.

Visit your local Concord county office

programs.name: Aged, Blind, and Disabled
programs.description: Coverage for seniors 65+ in Concord with disabilities
programs.eligibility: Income below SSI limits

faqs.question: What are the Medi-Cal income limits in Concord, CA?
faqs.answer: In Concord, Medi-Cal income limits follow the statewide California guidelines — generally 138% of the Federal Poverty Level for most adults. Seniors 65+ may have different limits. Contact the Concord county office for exact figures.

faqs.question: Where can I apply for Medi-Cal in Concord?
faqs.answer: Apply online through the California Medicaid portal, by phone, or in person at the Concord county Department of Social Services. Processing typically takes 30-45 days.

faqs.question: Does Medi-Cal cover home care in Concord?
faqs.answer: Yes, Medi-Cal may cover home and community-based services in Concord through waiver programs. These allow eligible seniors to receive care at home rather than in a nursing facility.
